current_state: Runner exposes local_reference plus command/openai/anthropic/gemini provider routes.

current_state: External runner commands receive prompt path, task identity, scaffold, lookup command, and public-file metadata.
limitation: Static source checks do not prove a third-party adapter honors those variables.
next_action: Keep this contract stable and update adapter docs when adding runner-supplied fields.

current_state: Anthropic has a minimal bundled adapter; OpenAI and Gemini are supported through external command env vars, not dedicated committed adapters.
limitation: Missing dedicated adapters are acceptable for local report readiness but limit turnkey provider comparability.
next_action: Add dedicated OpenAI and Gemini adapters or document external runner commands before a broad frontier sweep.

current_state: The bundled Anthropic adapter reads credentials from the environment and emits Lean text.
limitation: This is a static audit; it does not call the provider API or validate current model availability.
next_action: Run a fresh smoke job with explicit cost/version notes before claiming provider performance.

current_state: Tracked files contain no high-confidence API key patterns or direct API-key assignments.
limitation: Pattern scanning can miss unknown key formats and should not replace careful review.
next_action: Keep credentials in environment variables only and rerun this audit before publishing.

current_state: The public documentation states the no-fake-results and env-var credential policy.
limitation: Policy text is not evidence that future provider rows are externally reproduced.
next_action: Require transcripts, run-result integrity, and provider-version notes for every future model run.

limitation: Transcript presence does not make the tiny smoke set representative.
